opening_message: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.
scenario_description: Borrower starts by giving a realistic payment update, then reveals unemployment, refuses to commit to payment, and resists pressure as the conversation continues.
borrower_profile: Borrower is financially stressed, frustrated, and increasingly resistant.
borrower_intent: Explain they cannot pay right now, avoid committing to payment, and push back if pressured.
stop_condition: Stop after the final notice stage or after the scripted follow-up messages are exhausted.
follow_up_messages: I am unemployed right now and I cannot commit to any payment.
I am not giving dates or agreeing to any plan.
This conversation is going nowhere and I am done discussing payment.

opening_message: I do want to clear this loan, but I cannot pay the full amount today.
scenario_description: Borrower is cooperative and wants to repay, but is dealing with a short-term cash flow constraint after an unexpected family medical expense and delayed salary credit. They can make a small payment now and a larger payment after their next salary date, but they need the agent to work within those limits.
borrower_profile: Borrower is polite, practical, and trying to find a realistic way to pay without overcommitting.
borrower_intent: Show willingness to pay, explain the temporary constraint clearly, avoid making promises they cannot keep, and try to agree to a realistic policy-compliant option such as a structured payment plan if the agent proposes one.
reply_style_rules: Do not agree to impossible dates or amounts.
If the agent offers a realistic plan, show willingness to consider it.
stop_condition: Stop after a clear payment arrangement is reached, or after the system pushes unrealistic terms repeatedly.
expected_path_notes: This should test whether the collections flow can gather the constraint, avoid unnecessary escalation, and move toward a realistic resolution path instead of forcing refusal or final notice.
follow_up_messages: I had a family medical expense this week and my salary is delayed, so I can only pay a small amount right now.
I can pay 2000 now and the rest after my salary comes in on the 10th, but I cannot commit to the full amount before that.
If there is a structured plan that fits that timing, I am willing to discuss it.

opening_message: I need to discuss this because your outstanding amount looks incorrect to me.
scenario_description: Borrower is calm and engaged but insists there is a mismatch between prior payments and the current balance shown. They seek reconciliation first before committing to payment.
borrower_profile: Borrower is detail-oriented, keeps payment references ready, and expects factual clarification before agreeing to any plan.
borrower_intent: Request ledger reconciliation, avoid committing to payment until discrepancy is explained, and stay open to next steps once clarity is provided.
reply_style_rules: Repeat that reconciliation is required before commitment.
If discrepancy is clarified convincingly, become cooperative.
stop_condition: Stop after either a clear reconciliation path is offered or after repeated pressure to pay without addressing the dispute.
expected_path_notes: Tests whether the flow can handle non-hardship disagreement, capture dispute context properly, and avoid premature negotiation pressure.
follow_up_messages: I made a payment last month and I do not see it reflected, so I cannot accept this total yet.
Please first confirm the ledger entries and the latest posted payment before asking for commitment.
If the records are corrected or clearly explained, I can discuss a payment timeline.

opening_message: I can close this quickly, but only if the terms are reasonable.
scenario_description: Borrower is not in hardship and has capacity to pay, but negotiates assertively for favorable closure terms and a concrete deadline before committing.
borrower_profile: Borrower is pragmatic, time-conscious, and compares options before deciding.
borrower_intent: Push for a better closure offer or clear plan terms, avoid immediate acceptance, and commit once a policy-aligned option is clearly defined.
reply_style_rules: Ask for concrete numbers and deadlines.
If a clear policy-compliant option is presented, move toward commitment.
stop_condition: Stop after explicit commitment is made on clear terms, or if no concrete terms are provided after repeated attempts.
expected_path_notes: Tests deal-structure clarity, policy-bounded negotiation, and whether the agent can convert a willing borrower without unnecessary escalation.
follow_up_messages: I can arrange funds this week, but I need exact closure terms before I commit.
If you can state the approved amount and deadline clearly, I can decide now.
Give me one concrete option I can execute today, otherwise I will revisit later.
